verses.text: The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
But they that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength.
This is the day which the Lord hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.
Be kindly affectioned one to another with brotherly love.
Behold, the former things are come to pass, and new things do I declare.
Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away.
The earth is the Lord's, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.
He is not here: for he is risen, as he said.
Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which according to his abundant mercy hath begotten us again unto a lively hope.
We know that all things work together for good to them that love God.
He causeth the grass to grow for the cattle, and herb for the service of man.
The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork.
O give thanks unto the Lord; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
The Lord is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.
Thou openest thine hand, and satisfiest the desire of every living thing.
Behold, God is my salvation; I will trust, and not be afraid.
Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, and cometh down from the Father of lights.
Delight thyself also in the Lord; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
Be of good courage, and he shall strengthen your heart, all ye that hope in the Lord.
Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments.
I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
Whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men.
Teach me thy way, O Lord; I will walk in thy truth.
Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving, and make a joyful noise unto him with psalms.
The earth is the Lord's, and the fulness thereof.
Sing unto the Lord with thanksgiving; sing praise upon the harp unto our God.
Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise.
O give thanks unto the Lord, for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body.
For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given.
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.
Thanks be unto God for his unspeakable gift.
